description:
IS Markets is the short form for IntelliSource Markets B.V., a company whose very logo proclaims that it offers “Institutional Prices for Retail Clients”. Many people are already aware that the Foreign Exchange (Forex) market is the largest financial market in the world. It is also one that appears to allow both institutions and individuals to trade currency pairs on a roughly equal footing.
As indicated above IS Markets’ mission is made fairly obvious by its website, yet its background history is not found there at all. Two links at the top of the page let us know that the company operates out of the Netherlands and also Nevis, B.V. I. (British Virgin Islands). This is nice to know, but a bit more history might also help.
IS Markets’ services include a second tier prime broker service. We are assured that the company has relationships with some of the largest banks currently trading in Forex as well. The company also offers retail trading services. The website clearly spells out the minimum amounts required of a retail trader and a margin rate on Forex accounts of 70%. A Standard Account lot is US$100,000.
If a client has Forex trading experience or knowledge this sort of disclosure or candor is refreshing and somewhat reassuring. IS Markets uses MetaTrader 4 as its platform, which covers all brokerage and trading activities at Forex. Other web-based brokers use that platform as well.
A special section of the website labeled EDUCATION provides a good summary of the basics of Forex trading. An extensive reading list is provided for further study and a glossary is available at the website for instant definitions of Forex terms.
If you are ready to get started trading you can select a link on the main IS Markets page to open a live account. Set up software for a free demo account is also available there.

Once one has navigated to KBC’s website, chosen the language of one’s choice and selected “Group Profile” under “About KBC” one can become rather quickly informed as to what KBC is all about in general.
KBC is a self described “integrated bancassurance group”. It caters mainly to retail customers, small and medium-sized enterprises and private banking clientele. It is a market leader in its home markets of Belgium and Central and Eastern Europe. KBC specializes there in retail bancassurance and asset management.
The term bancassurance typically describes a partnership between a bank and an insurance company whereby the insurance company uses the bank’s sales channels to sell insurance products.
The company has its origins in 1998 when two Belgian banks (the Kredietbank and CERA Bank) and a Belgian insurance company (ABB) merge to create the KBC Bank and Insurance Holding Company. Over the years KBC continued to expand its operations in Central and Eastern Europe by acquiring banks and insurance companies in Poland, Hungary, the Czech Republic and Slovakia.
In North America KBC Financial Products offers expertise in the following customer businesses: Equities, Convertible Bonds and Derivatives.
In an equities context KBC Financial Products is known for its equity agency execution services on the Tokyo Stock Exchange for globally based clients. Convertible bond activities include acting as a market maker in global convertible bonds through one of the leading sales, trading and research teams globally.
This is a customer-driven business, with dedicated teams in London, New York and Hong Kong providing 24 hour coverage of the markets. Participation in derivatives includes acting as a market leader in innovative equity derivative products in Asia as well as involvement in Europe trading three main product areas - single index products, index correlation products and single stock and single basket products.
A broker or someone familiar with the broad scope of financial and insurance services it offers on a worldwide basis might best initiate individual involvement with KBC.

The services offered by Keytrade Bank are more varied and complex than many other financially related websites and this is reflected in the website itself. Selecting “About Keytrade” at the very top of the page is perhaps safer initially than picking “Who are we?” a little lower and to the left. The latter selection initiates a video presentation in French with English subtitles and musical accompaniment.
In brief: Belgium's first online investment site VMS-Keytrade began in 1998. In 2002, VMS-Keytrade became Keytrade Bank and acquired its banking status by taking over RealBank.
Keytrade Bank offers its clients investment fund services, portfolio services that allow clients to customize fund savings plans, as well as an online trading platform for Forex - Futures - CFD’s. The aforementioned trading platform can be tested free for up to 20 days. Forex services include extended hours trading and access to financial news and charts. Those two information streams are considered crucial for successful trading in the volatile Forex market.
Keytrade Bank also keeps its investment and trading customers informed via newsletters and warning and notification services that could be email or SMS based. Support services also include permanent assistance to commercial service clients by way of telephone and Skype.
Keytrade Bank's banking services include current accounts, savings accounts and term accounts. A full range of insurance services is also available from Keytrade Bank.
The company has rates for all of its services clearly posted under a separate tab labeled “Prices”. Keytrade Bank has also invested a considerable amount of its resources in keeping its potential and existing customers informed about all aspects of its operations. Featured on the Keytrade Bank site is access to many training and informational videos, a frequently asked questions section and a Document Center that makes all the relevant forms available in PDF file format in English, Dutch and French.

description:
Varengold is a German Investment Bank that stands for excellence and sustainable value in all they do and have aimed at this since their establishment. They were awarded best German fund of hedge funds in 2007 and 2008 in Germany. The size of the company and their state of the art technology enables their global reach. With support offices in China and Switzerland, they have 7000 clients globally.
Founded in 1995, Varengold was the foremost German Investment Banker to focus solely on Managed Futures, Hedge Finds and Alternative Investments. This company has a great reputation in the field and is still the exclusive specialist in Managed Futures and Hedge Funds for midsized institutional and private investors. Varengold still uses its In-House Managed Futures Strategies that it developed in the early years after inception to act mainly as a Fund of Funds Manager. It uses its Capital Markets’ Brokerage Segment to manage risk, both its own and that of its clients.
In terms of Asset Management, the HI Varengold CTA Hedge provides investors with stable returns not correlated to stocks and bonds, and is an award winning fund. In the area of Brokerage, this company deals with a wide range of services provided in Foreign Exchange, Contracts for Difference (CFDs), Futures, Options and Hedging. In terms of Foreign Exchange, an interactive platform, Varengold Fox Trader, is provided on its website for clients to deal in forex from their computer or cell phone. There is high portfolio diversification, 1:100 leverage and high volumes, 24-hours a day service, and current information to support decisions. With CFDs, Varengold has 10 years experience and an easy platform as well as expert employees. Their 13 years’ experience gives customers the security they need in the field of futures. They have dealt in options since the company began and trading is easily done over the phone; this is also an area of expertise for this company.
Varengold moves a 100 million Euros daily. Varengold measures its success by the yardsticks of integrity and fairness. This website provides ample information for those wishing to get acquainted with the company as well as the various arenas in which it provides service. It also highlights why this company is a great choice for potential and established investors.
The hallmarks of Varengold are excellence, matchless speed, 24-hour assistance, web-based trading with no download, superior exchange rates and no commissions, all of which inspire the clients’ trust.

Master Forex has been helping people with wider horizons earn money on financial markets since 2nd October, 2006. Their emphasis on quality and professionalism coupled with their capital base makes for some very satisfied customers. The two primary goals of this company are managing the assets of its clients in 130 countries worldwide, and providing them easy access to the world’s financial markets.
The dynamism of Master Forex is evidence by all the recent changes made, for instance, considering their customer’s views and opening up a 24-hour Customer Service department to improve their quality of service, developing a network of representative offices in the countries in which Master Forex operates, and the establishment of a Representative Department which smoothes things over for all the branches and facilitates their cohesive operation.
Master Forex offers clients high quality projections and various facilities. Since Forex is not a market with strict rules and opening times and all operations are made on the basis of free currency exchange, even small conversions get to be quite expensive. This is where institutions such as Master Forex come in.
The merits of working with this company are many, among these:
- Minimum deposit of $1
- Freedom of creating one’s own future and augmenting income
- Forex tools
- Excellent Affiliate program
- Leverage up to 1:500
- Spread from two points
- Speed of execution.
MF Trader 4 is the Trading platform used by Master Forex and is one of the best available. It allows technical analysis and managing trading operations in real time while working on the Forex, Futures and Contracts for Difference markets.
This website is convenient to navigate and ensures that beginners are well-informed, and more seasoned clients are well taken care of. Anyone can school themselves on the basics of Master Forex, its procedures, its mission statement, as well as have access to the latest data and analyses. There is a section where one can ask questions that are responded to with speed and also contests that are held.
Provision is made to enter ones personal history of trading and all other details so a clear record is maintained and can be managed online. All of this shows adherence to the company’s mission of providing the best service and that it values its customers above all else.

pros: Competitive regulation and maximum leverage; Choice of trading platforms for copy traders; Quality educational section for beginner traders

cons: Limited or no explanation for reason behind trades; Daily updates giving signal entries/exits does not arrive at exactly the same time every day
pros: Profitable over two and a half week testing period; Suitable for traders who have no time or access during the working day (Europe/ME/Africa); “Set and Forget” orders big enough to avoid spread/slippage issues

description: For Forex trading, KGI Futures offers a choice of two trading platforms. One is the traditional, very popular MetaTrader 4 including web, mobile, and desktop access. This platform is the most familiar to many Forex traders and as a result is the first choice of many. The other option that KGI offers is called MaxxTrader. They claim this platform is highly configurable and is comprehensive and complete.

ACM Gold LTD is a Forex broker and authorized financial service provider based in South Africa. The brokerage firm is one of few Forex brokers that accepts transactions in South African rands. They have won several awards from Global Finance Magazine over the last few years. The brokerage uses the MetaTrader 4 platform to offer trading to its clients on most devices throughout the day and night. ACM Gold allows customers to trade all major Forex pairs as well as a number of minor crosses and exotic pairs. Their list of instruments also includes Forex CFDs, commodities, world shares, metals, energies and futures.

description: Fullerton Markets is a New Zealand-based Forex brokerage that claims to be the fastest growing broker in Asia. The company was founded by Mario Singh, a Forex industry veteran who previously worked at FXPRIMUS, another large Asian brokerage. The firm is very new so there is very little information available about their customer satisfaction history. The current discussion in online forums seems to be quite positive.
pros: مجموعة واسعة من الأصول; تداول الأسهم على نطاق واسع

description: CIMB Securities is a securities brokerage firm based in Singapore with offices in Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand, Hong Kong, London and the United States that services for trading in equities and derivatives. A review of this firm’s website tells us very little background information about the firm. The website seems to be geared towards professional traders. CIMB is predominantly an online institutional and retail broker focusing mostly on stocks.

cons: High Spreads; High Minimum Deposit
description: Credit Financier Invest, launched in 2009, is something quite different from the typical Forex broker. It is a high-end subsidiary of a Lebanese Bank, in a jurisdiction famous for its banking services, known formerly as the “Switzerland of the Middle East”. The Credit Financier group has been in existence for decades, and is regulated by the Bank of Lebanon. This is a broker with a high though non-traditional regulatory reputation. However, it should be noted right away that with a minimum deposit requirement of $5,000, opening an account with Credit Financier Invest will not be an option for every trader.
pros: Wide Range of Assets; Middle Eastern Location

description: Larson & Holz IT Ltd is a Forex broker headquartered in St. Vincent and the Grenadines with satellite offices in many other countries. Since their establishment in 2004, their goal has been to provide easy internet access to financial markets all over the world. They very clearly state on the website that they do not provide asset management or guarantee profits and that their franchise offices are independent companies. They do not provide services to citizens of the US, North Korea and other specific countries based on those countries regulations. We found during our Larson & Holz review that this firm is registered under the Centre for Regulation in OTC Financial Instruments and Technologies (CRFIN) as well as a member of the Commission for the Regulation of Relations in the Financial Markets (KROUFR).

NPFBX is a Forex brokerage that used to be part of a Russian commercial bank and has now become its own entity offering trading services regulated off shore, except for institutional clients who are still served by the parent body. NBPFX’s parent bank is regulated by the Bank of Russia, and claims to be operating since 1996. The current retail Forex brokerage is regulated by the International Financial Services Commission in Belize. When the new company was formed it became part of the Financial Commission and then established a compensation fund of $20,000 per customer. The website is available in English, French, Vietnamese, Malaysian, Russian, and Indonesian and their target market seems to be Russian speakers.

NPFBX has a low minimum deposit of only $10. They offer three different account types: Master, Expert and VIP. The minimum deposit for a Master account is $10 and with that account users can trade with a leverage of 1:500 and an average spread of 1.2 pips. The Expert account requires a deposit of at least $5000 and allows traders leverage of 1:100 and an average spread of 1.0 pips. A VIP will have to deposit $50,000 and will trade with an average spread of 0.8 pips and leverage of 1:100. Aside from the leverage and minimum deposits and trade volume, the accounts have almost no difference. Each account holder is able to trade 36 currency pairs, Gold, Silver, Oil and natural gas.

This Forex broker offers customer support 25 hours a day, 5 days a week in several languages. The website has a chat function as well as a live support phone line. NPFBX has many different avenues for deposit and withdrawal with instructions for each on their website.

The popular MetaTrader 4 is the trading platform of choice for NPFBX. The website lists the advantages to this platform and it is easy to access. In addition, is integrated with ZuluTrade which allows customers to choose and follow successful traders within the platform. This platform can be a great combination of one’s own trading strategy and the strategies of other successful traders. Traders who wish to use mirror trading can utilize the MyFxbook Auto Trade platform through NPFBX. It is an advanced mirror trading platform that can be used by customers of this brokerage and it is supported by the MyFxbook staff.

One thing that NPFBX does not seem to offer is educational resources. Their website has very little under the heading of education. Traders who want to learn via using a demo account are welcome but there are no lessons, videos, ebooks or other educational resources that other Forex brokers generally provide.

The online reviews of NPFBX are basically favorable. Ironically, their website itself does not have testimonials but other websites that review Forex brokers seem to have positive things to say about this company. The user comments are positive and complimentary which is not something to take lightly. Though we question why the brokerage, based in Russia, would be regulated by a body located across the world, we do appreciate that regulation exists, and that traders do seem satisfied with the service. Don’t be afraid to try a demo account before trading with NBPFX – it’s research time that may be important to your financial future.

EVERFX is a Forex brokerage that offers online trading of Forex and CFDs. The official name of the business is ICC Intercertus Capital Limited and they are licensed and regulated by the Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC). In addition, the company is registered to offer certain products in the UK. EVERFX is also registered in Spain and Poland as a foreign investment company. Although the brokerage has only been licensed since 2016, it does offer quite an impressive list of international registrations. Clients funds are held in segregated accounts and are kept by the company separate from its own funds.

Royal Financial Trading, known online as RFXT.com is a licensed financial trading firm with offices in Cyprus, Australia and Lebanon. They are licensed by the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC), Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CYSEC) and the Capital Markets Authority (CMA) and Banque Du Libon of Lebanon. These regulations should provide protection to traders who want to make sure their deposits are protected.

cons: No Bonuses; No Education Offering
description: Formax Prime Capital, founded in 2017 by a parent company with a background in the online brokerage industry, is a spread-betting and CFD broker headquartered and regulated in the United Kingdom. They state that they operate a ECN (electronic communications network) model, using STP (straight through processing) technology, without any dealing desk interference. A wide range of assets for trading are offered, in over 60 Forex currency pairs, 11 major stock indices, and a few major commodities (gold, silver, crude oil, and natural gas).
pros: Competitive Spreads; Deposit Insurance by FSMA

description: FTO Capital, Financials Trading Online is a Forex broker that is incorporated in the Republic of the Marshall Islands and their office is located in the UK. The brokerage seems to have opened in March of 2017 and remains unlicensed and unregulated as of the time of this FTO review, which is a very good reason to avoid trading with this broker. The website is available in English and Russian.

Darwinex, in operation since 2012 and located in the UK, is a technology provider that enables traders direct market access to over 300 assets. Their prime service remains in asset management, allowing traders to invest in thousands of strategies, while also being able to legally gain access to trading capital for a 15% performance fee. An algorithmic risk manager system classifies distinct strategies, each of which is called a DARWIN; the DARWINs are traded in the same manner as any other assets. Every month, Darwinex invests €6 million of its funds into 80 DARWINs (those with the highest returns), for a duration of up to six months. Its prime brokers are Saxo Bank and LMAX, and Darwinex grants a secure trading environment. Overall, the business model is rather new, as evidenced by just over 3,000 traders from over 80 countries. Darwinex paid out over $2.8 million in performance fees and has a promising approach to significantly increase its market share.

cons: No Choice of Platform; No Bonuses or Promotions
description: Key to Markets, a Mauritius-based broker founded in 2010, claims to offer access to the electronic communication network (ECN) via the MT4 trading platform. Two subsidiaries, one in the UK and one in New Zealand, complete the group structure. The UK-based entity operates as a broker which caters to traders from the European Economic Area (EEA). The New Zealand entity is not a broker; it merely owns the MT4 trading platform, which it distributes to the two brokerage units. Key to Markets claims to offer a wide range of products but lists only 167 assets. Other claims include direct market access (DMA) and no dealing desk (NDD) execution. Social trading and retail account management are also available at this broker. Statistics show that 71.0% of retail traders lose money at Key to Markets.
pros: Competitive Spreads; Wide Range of Assets

description: Alfa Financial is a Dubai-based Forex brokerage that was established in 1993. According to their website they are licensed and regulated by the Central Bank of the U.A.E., Dubai Chamber of Commerce and Industry and the Economic Department of Dubai although there are some online reviews that make claims to the contrary. Alfa Financial claims that they carefully check into every product and clearing firm that they offer to customers and that all are approved by the Central Bank of the U.A.E. Alfa Financial does not manage the accounts themselves, they are offer intermediary services between their clients and international clearing houses.

cons: Still a relatively new broker
description: TradeTime makes a number of bold statements, including the assertion that they are both an industry leader and innovator. The broker further makes that claim that it has been in operation for seven years, is regulated, and segregates accounts. Unfortunately, TradeTime is unable to back up its assertions; in nearly every case it fails to provide details and/or proof. At the same time, it focuses exclusively on marketing its proprietary trading platform and account customization, both of which come at an elevated cost, that is unless a trader is willing to make a substantial deposit.
pros: Customized Trading Accounts; Choice of Platforms; Suited more for starters

cons: No Demo Account; No Bonuses or Promotions

Under the brand name of Growell Capital, it is authorized and regulated by the Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC).

cons: No Choice of Account Types; Little Material on Platforms
description: TFI Markets Ltd, founded in 1999, and domiciled in Nicosia, Cyprus, is a CySec regulated financial institution, providing multi-platform currency trading for its corporate clientele. It is also EU licensed by MiFid, CBC, BaFin and AMF. TFI was one of the pioneers of the concept of currency trading by launching its TFIFX platform which targeted professional and experienced clients. Since 2009 it has also offered FX margin trading services.
pros: Unique Options; High Leverage; No Transaction Costs

description: JFD is based in Cyprus and operates under European regulations. They are regulated and licensed by CySEC in Cyprus, BaFin in Germany, the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) in the United Kingdom, CONSOB in Italy, and the ACPR in France. The sheer number of regulatory licenses this broker holds is impressive, and it should be a good sign of the broker’s reliability.

cons: No Scalping; No Choice of Platforms
description: Vestle UK is a trading name of Black Pearl Securities Limited (Company number 08823678). Black Pearl Securities Limited is authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA). They offer traders the opportunity to trade an unusually wide range of CFDs (Contracts for Difference), even including a selection of ETFs (exchange-traded funds). Access to such a wide-ranging investment universe is unusual in the retail online brokerage industry. In addition to ETFs, Vestle offers CFD trading on commodities, indices, shares, cryptocurrencies, and Forex currency pairs across both major and minor financial markets. A total of over 800 instruments can be traded with Vestle.

description: Khwezi Trade (a division of Khwezi Financial Services FSP 44816) is a relatively new online Forex and CFD brokerage launched in 2014. They are headquartered and regulated in the Republic of South Africa by the FSCA. They stand out as a uniquely South African brokerage and not just a sales office of a multinational which has secured regulation there. They offer traders the opportunity to trade a reasonably wide range of instruments wrapped as CFDs (Contracts for Difference), even including a selection of soft commodities. Khwezi Trade offer trading in 60 forex currency pairs, several commodities including softs, and the major global equity indices. All accounts and trades may be denominated in South African Rand.
pros: Denominated in South African Rand; Wide Selection of Assets

description: Forex trading is becoming increasingly popular in South Africa, so it’s no surprise that Forex brokerages are popping up in the country left and right. The good news is that there are very strict regulations in South Africa so it is possible to find a broker that will keep your money segregated and protected. The FSB, Financial Services Board monitors the actions of the brokerages in South Africa and offers regulation for JP Markets. According to its website, JP Markets has offices in Kenya, Pakistan and Bangladesh, though we haven’t visited them to ascertain the reliability of these claims.

cons: U.S. Traders Not Accepted; High Minimum Deposit
description: Saxo Capital Markets, the Australian subsidiary of Danish investment bank, Saxo Bank, was founded as a brokerage in 1992. In 2012, Saxo Capital Market became operational but just two days later it was reprimanded by the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) over its risk management practices. After a rocky start, the online brokerage swiftly expanded, largely as a result of its competitive product and services portfolio. Saxo Capital Markets maintains over 40,000 assets, placing it in the top two spots among all online brokers globally, but traders must commit to a minimum deposit of A$3,000. Unlike other ASIC-regulated brokers which offer a maximum leverage of 1:500, Saxo Capital Markets caps it at 1:50. With more than 600,000 clients, it remains one of the most competitive Australian online brokers.

description: Advanced Currency Markets (ACM) was founded in 2001 and is the Forex broker branch of the Swissquote Group, which it acquired in October 2010. It is regulated by Finma and registered as a spot Forex broker in the Seychelles. Although Swissquote is a market maker broker, ACM-FX is an ECN Forex broker and is not a dealing desk.

description: IFGM is an Australian-based Forex broker. They are part of Intelligent Financial Markets Pty Ltd group. From their beginning in 2012, IFGM has been regulated by the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) and licensed by Australian Financial Services. They strictly abide by Australian Client Money Rules and hold client funds separately at the National Australia Bank. Knowing that they are carefully regulated and monitored definitely can give customers more confidence when choosing a Forex broker.

InstaForex LTD was founded in 2007 and over the past twelve years has become home to over 7,000,000 traders. This brokerage is registered in the British Virgin Islands but retains offices in over 260 countries with core staff in Russia. Traders have access to over 300 assets, and InstaForex LTD remains known for sponsorships across professional sports as well as trading competitions with an annual prize pool of $500,000; this year’s prize pool stands at $1,000,000. Over 30 analysts are employed by this broker who was awarded more than 30 financial awards in recent years. The maximum leverage given is 1:1000, there is no minimum deposit, and bonuses are offered with each deposit. Terms & conditions apply but are acceptable, and traders can collect their bonuses over time and withdraw them.

Exness is a Forex broker that was founded by a group of finance and information technology experts in 2008. On October 28th, 2019, the company announced the closure of its UK and Cyprus regulated retail operations. As such, information displayed on the respective domains (dot-UK and dot-EU) is no longer applicable, and new account openings are unavailable. The data provided contradicted itself and was flawed, countering the claims of transparency. Exness was, prior to the closures, compliant with its regulators. The broker is now attempting to focus on white label solutions. The Seychelles regulated subsidiary, under the name Nymstar Limited, remains operational in the retail sector; it is currently accepting new accounts, and is accessible under the dot-com domain.

ZERO Markets is the trade name of ZERO Financial Pty Ltd, which is an authorized representative of First Prudential Markets Pty Ltd. It has been in operation since 2017, according to the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC), its regulatory body. As the name suggests, this CFD broker offers raw spreads to its traders, features a proprietary trading platform and, being ASIC regulated, a very competitive trading environment. ZERO Markets is a White Label solution of First Prudential Markets (which is also the authorized representative of FPMarkets); all products are designed and maintained by them. The asset selection covers five sectors, and account management services are available via MAM/PAMM accounts.

cons: No demo account
description: Spreadex, founded in 1999, is a UK-based and regulated spread betting, CFD trading, and sports betting firm. Traders get a proprietary, web-based trading platform and can connect to TradingView. Spreadex features excellent order execution and prides itself on superb customer support. Therefore, I consider Spreadex an excellent choice for UK-based spread betters. My Spreadex review evaluated its trading environment. Is Spreadex the right broker for you?
pros: No minimum deposit requirement; Spread betting, CFD trading, and sports betting; A well-balanced asset selection of 1,000+ trading instruments

description: TD Ameritrade is a US-based broker but, unlike most of its US counterparts, it accepts international clients. Founded in 1971 as Ameritrade, in 2006 the company acquired TD Waterhouse from the Canadian financial conglomerate, Toronto-Dominion Bank, which granted it a 40% minority stake. In 2020, the Charles Schwab Corporation acquired TD Ameritrade, which gave Toronto-Dominion Bank 13% ownership of Charles Schwab. The acquisition created an online brokerage powerhouse in the US. TD Ameritrade has over 12,000,000 client accounts worth over $1.2 trillion, and more than 2,100,000 trades are performed each day. Traders have access to the proprietary thinkorswim trading platform, with tens of thousands of assets across multiple sectors and a focus on equity, ETF, and mutual funds.

description: Robinhood Markets, founded in 2013, is a pioneer in commission-free trading. In 2015, this US-based financial services company officially launched its trading app. Making financial markets accessible to all traders without additional costs remains the mission of Robinhood, which has over 13,000,000 users according to its latest SEC filing. Robinhood caters primarily to millennial traders with an average age of 26. It also expanded into cryptocurrency trading via Robinhood Crypto. Another attractive feature is fractional shares trading, eliminating the need for massive deposits to trade blue-chip equities. Robinhood does sell its order book to high-frequency trading firms, which prompted the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to launch a probe in September 2020. Robinhood remains in high demand among new retail traders, many of whom generally have little to no knowledge of financial markets. Nearly half of all Robinhood traders claim to use it daily, and 90% at least weekly; most traders prefer to trade from their mobile phones.
pros: Quality education for beginner traders; Balanced asset selection for equity and cryptocurrency traders; A dedicated cryptocurrency account; User-friendly and highly rated mobile app
